name: Apollo 13

parentage.notes: The commonly documented Brothers Grimm line; other plants have circulated under the name.

overview: Apollo 13 is remembered as a fast-finishing, high-character Brothers Grimm hybrid with an outsized reputation among collectors. Growers value its unusual aromatic range, energetic profile, and usefulness in breeding. Its scarcity and the existence of later recreations make version and provenance especially important when evaluating a plant labeled Apollo 13.

aroma.phenotypeVariation: Expressions are reported from tropical and citrus-forward to sharper solvent and incense profiles.

effects.clarity: Clear at moderate intake; more disorienting with higher intake

cultivation.trainingResponse: Often responds well to topping and lateral spreading
cultivation.outdoor: Best in a long, dry finish

phenotypes.name: Tropical/solvent
phenotypes.aroma: Sharp tropical fruit with citrus solvent

breedingValue.notes: Inheritance claims vary by parent and selection; evaluate progeny rather than assuming dominance.

history.summary: Associated with the original Brothers Grimm catalog and later preservation or recreation efforts.
disputes: Modern offerings may not represent the same release or parental selections.
history.reputation: A breeder and collector favorite with a strong old-school reputation.

confidence.notes: Core lineage is well documented; sensory and cultivation details vary across preserved and recreated versions.

name: Space Queen
overview: Space Queen became a durable breeding building block because it can combine Romulan depth with the brighter fruit and vigor associated with Cinderella 99. Different selections can feel substantially different, so the breeder, generation, and chosen parents matter. Collectors often value expressive fruit, resin, branching, and a lively but grounded character.

aroma.phenotypeVariation: Some plants lean toward bright fruit and citrus; others show deeper pine, earth, and spice.

breedingValue.notes: Outcomes depend heavily on the Space Queen selection and partner.

history.summary: Created by Vic High and later used in numerous projects by other breeders.
history.reputation: Respected as a versatile old-school breeding line.

confidence.notes: Lineage is widely accepted; version, generation, and selected parents can differ.

name: Cinderella 99

parentage.notes: The shorthand reflects the documented Brothers Grimm breeding sequence; simplified parent labels vary by archive.

overview: Cinderella 99 earned an enduring reputation for combining a compact indoor-friendly frame and comparatively short flowering window with bright, high-energy character. Pineapple, citrus, floral, and solvent-like expressions are frequently discussed. Original releases, later preservation lines, and modern reproductions should be treated as related but not automatically identical.

aroma.phenotypeVariation: Reported expressions range from pineapple candy to sharper floral-solvent profiles.

cultivation.trainingResponse: Good response to topping and an even canopy

phenotypes.name: Pineapple
phenotypes.aroma: Sweet fermented pineapple and mango candy

history.summary: An influential Brothers Grimm line derived through work with the Princess clone and selected male parents.
disputes: Modern seed lines may use different preservation stock or reconstruction methods.
history.reputation: A foundational modern breeding parent and well-known indoor cultivar.

confidence.notes: Historical sequence is documented, while shorthand lineage labels and modern versions vary.

name: Vortex
overview: Vortex sits at the intersection of Apollo and Space Queen families and is often remembered for electric fruit, citrus-cleaner aromas, resin, and a fast-moving cerebral profile. It became a recognizable part of the TGA catalog and a useful reference point for later fruit-forward breeding. Individual seed releases and surviving cuts may not represent one uniform expression.

aroma.phenotypeVariation: Plants may lean toward sharp lemon-cleaner, tropical candy, or a deeper Space Queen fruit-and-spice direction.

effects.clarity: Bright initially; potentially disorienting at higher intake

cultivation.trainingResponse: Often suitable for topping and lateral spreading

breedingValue.notes: Treat claimed inheritance as selection-specific rather than guaranteed.

history.summary: Released through TGA Subcool and later used in related catalog projects.
history.reputation: A recognizable TGA-era cultivar with strong collector interest.

confidence.notes: Core lineage is widely reported; exact release and selection details need archive review.

name: Jack's Cleaner

parentage.display: Complex selected line; exact shorthand varies
parentage.notes: Often associated with Cleaner, Jack Herer, and Pluton-related ancestry; simplified retellings differ.

overview: Jack's Cleaner is an old-school selection known in breeder and collector circles for penetrating lemon-cleaner character, resin, and a vivid cerebral profile. Its long-form breeding history is often compressed inconsistently, so the specific cut and source should be recorded rather than relying only on a short lineage string.

aroma.phenotypeVariation: The named cut is more specific than seed-derived descendants.

breedingValue.notes: Breeding observations depend on the authenticated cut and chosen counterpart.

history.summary: A significant Subcool-associated selection used in later TGA work.
history.reputation: Collector-grade clone and important lemon-cleaner reference.

confidence.notes: The cut is recognized, but published ancestry shorthand varies and needs source-by-source review.

name: Jillybean
overview: Jillybean is associated with a bright citrus and fruit-candy profile, a sociable character, and colorful, expressive plants. It is often discussed as a friendly entry point to the Space Queen family, though phenotype spread and differing releases mean growers should keep careful notes on source and selection.

aroma.phenotypeVariation: Orange, mango, and mixed-candy expressions are commonly reported.

confidence.notes: Sensory and growth summaries combine historical catalog language with grower reports.

name: Agent Orange
overview: Agent Orange is a citrus-led TGA-era hybrid valued for orange-rind intensity, vigor, and an upbeat daytime direction. Plants and later releases can vary, so a keeper is usually selected for persistent orange aroma, manageable stretch, flower quality, and balanced energy rather than name alone.

confidence.notes: Release-specific details and phenotype frequencies need verification.

name: Querkle
overview: Querkle is a purple-fruit hybrid that paired the clone-only reputation of Purple Urkle with Space Queen. It is often valued for grape, berry, earth, color potential, and a calmer finish. Selection remains important because plants can separate toward either parent's aroma, structure, timing, and effect character.

confidence.notes: Phenotype and cultivation details vary across seed lots.

name: Blueberry

parentage.display: Afghani × Thai × other selected landrace-derived lines
parentage.notes: The historical breeding population is more complex than a single two-parent shorthand.

overview: Blueberry is a foundational DJ Short line with long-term influence on modern fruit, color, and hybrid breeding. The name is now used broadly, but authenticated or well-preserved lines are valued for layered berry, floral, spice, and earthy notes rather than simple sweetness alone. Structural variation and occasional unusual leaf expressions are part of its collector lore.

cultivation.stressSensitivity: Some expressions are reported as sensitive

weaknesses: structural and sex-expression variability reported in some stock
breedingValue.notes: Use verified stock and document the individual parent carefully.

history.summary: Developed by DJ Short over multiple generations from selected landrace-derived material.
disputes: Modern strains sharing the name may be unrelated or only loosely derived.
history.reputation: One of the most influential named fruit families in modern cannabis breeding.

confidence.notes: Broad history is documented; exact shorthand and modern provenance require careful review.

sources.label: DJ Short historical writings and interviews

name: Northern Lights #5
breeder: Historical line; later associated with The Seed Bank/Sensi Seeds

parentage.notes: Detailed ancestry and numbering are historically contested.

overview: Northern Lights #5 is a central reference point for compact indoor structure, resin, and a deep relaxing profile. Its influence runs through countless hybrids, especially NL5/Haze work. Because the name spans early American material, European releases, and later reproductions, provenance is essential and simplified lineage claims should be treated cautiously.

confidence.notes: Cultivar reputation is strong; exact ancestry, numbering, and version histories remain contested.

name: Skunk #1

parentage.notes: This is the widely repeated historical shorthand; selection and preservation histories vary.

overview: Skunk #1 is one of the foundational stabilized hybrid populations of modern cannabis breeding. It is valued less as a single modern flavor stereotype than as a historically important source of vigor, uniformity, adaptable structure, and sweet-to-musky aromatic variation. Later lines selected in different directions should not be assumed to represent identical stock.

confidence.notes: Historical shorthand is widely accepted, while surviving versions differ.

name: Durban Poison

parentage.notes: Modern commercial versions may include selection or hybridization beyond original regional material.

overview: Durban Poison is a globally recognized name for South African-derived cannabis selected for energetic character, anise-like spice, and practical flowering compared with some tropical lines. The label now covers multiple versions, so a modern packet, preserved line, and regional heirloom should not be treated as interchangeable without documentation.

cultivation.trainingResponse: Can benefit from early canopy planning
cultivation.outdoor: Best matched to climate and finish window

confidence.notes: Regional origin is central to the name, but commercial versions have distinct histories.

name: Jack Herer

parentage.notes: Published shorthand does not always specify the exact sequence or selections.

overview: Jack Herer is a landmark Dutch-era hybrid that brought Haze character into a more practical Northern Lights and Skunk framework. Multiple phenotype directions are part of its reputation, from taller incense-and-citrus plants to denser, resinous expressions. The cultivar's historical importance and broad name recognition have also led to unrelated or weakly documented versions.

cultivation.trainingResponse: Can respond well to canopy management

confidence.notes: Core family ancestry is widely published; exact parental sequence and phenotype frequencies vary.

name: Chem 91

parentage.notes: Associated with the historic Chemdog seed story; no verified two-parent pedigree is available.

overview: Chem 91 is a foundational clone associated with the emergence of the Chem, Diesel, and related modern fuel families. Its influence is better documented through named cuts and descendants than through a verified pedigree. Provenance matters because several Chem 91 labels and selections have circulated over time.

breedingValue.notes: Results reported for one verified cut should not be generalized to every Chem 91 label.

history.summary: Part of the historic Chemdog clone family emerging from seeds acquired in 1991.
disputes: Several cuts and naming conventions exist.

confidence.notes: The clone's cultural history is well established, but its genetic parents are unknown.

name: Chem D

parentage.notes: Exact genetic parents are unknown.

overview: Chem D is a major Chem-family clone known for forceful fuel, savory funk, resin, and breeding influence. It is often treated as a reference expression for the family, but the plant's authenticated cut history is more reliable than any claimed conventional pedigree.

confidence.notes: Cut identity is historically important; genetic parentage remains unknown.

name: Chem 4

parentage.notes: Exact genetic parents are unknown.

overview: Chem 4 is a named Chem-family clone frequently distinguished by a somewhat brighter, lemon-fuel presentation and robust plant frame compared with other Chem selections. The specific cut is the meaningful unit of identity; seed products using the name should be documented separately.

confidence.notes: Parentage is unknown and growth descriptions depend on authenticated cut provenance.

name: Sour Diesel

parentage.display: Disputed; commonly linked to Chem/Diesel family material
parentage.notes: Multiple origin accounts and versions circulate.

overview: Sour Diesel is a defining East Coast clone name associated with piercing fuel, sour citrus, extended flowering, and a soaring cerebral character. Its reputation is secure, but ancestry and the relationship between Diesel, ECSD, and later seed versions remain disputed. Accurate records should distinguish an authenticated cut from a Sour Diesel-named seed line.

cultivation.trainingResponse: Can benefit from strong canopy control

confidence.notes: Cultivar identity and reputation are well established; ancestry and version boundaries remain disputed.

name: OG Kush

parentage.notes: Competing origin stories link OG Kush to Chem-family, regional Kush, and other material; no single pedigree is universally verified.

overview: OG Kush is a foundational West Coast clone family associated with lemon-pine fuel, dense resinous flowers, and a strong layered effect. The name now includes numerous cuts, hybrids, and unrelated products. A record should identify the exact cut—such as Tahoe, SFV, or another verified selection—before assigning detailed traits.

confidence.notes: Family traits are widely recognized; exact ancestry and cut identity require documentation.

name: Afghani #1
overview: Afghani #1 is a commercialized landrace-derived reference line associated with compact structure, broad leaves, dense resinous flowers, and hash-like aromas. It represents selected and maintained material rather than the full diversity of Afghan regional cannabis. Version and source matter when comparing historical and current stock.

confidence.notes: Afghan origin is central; exact preservation and selection history needs archive verification.

name: Thai

parentage.notes: Thai is a regional umbrella, not one uniform cultivar.

overview: Thai refers to diverse regional cannabis populations and preserved lines, many associated with long flowering, narrow leaves, tropical spice, incense, and a powerful cerebral character. Treating all Thai material as a single strain erases regional and selection differences. Records should capture the collection location, preservation generation, and maintainer wherever possible.

cultivation.trainingResponse: Requires early space and canopy planning indoors
cultivation.outdoor: Best in tropical or long-season climates

confidence.notes: Regional origin may be known for individual accessions; this umbrella record should not replace accession-level data.

name: Original Haze

parentage.display: Complex Colombian, Mexican, South Indian and Thai-associated ancestry
parentage.notes: Historical accounts differ on sequence, contributors, and surviving lines.

overview: Original Haze is a foundational long-flowering narrow-leaf family associated with incense, floral spice, soaring effects, and extraordinary breeding influence. Its history is intertwined with oral accounts and later preservation, making confident one-line pedigrees inappropriate. Exact seed source and maintainer are necessary to interpret any modern Haze record.

confidence.notes: Broad family history is accepted; detailed origin accounts remain contested.

name: Super Silver Haze

parentage.notes: Exact sequence and parent selections are not always described consistently.

overview: Super Silver Haze is a defining Dutch-era Haze hybrid combining classic incense and lift with more practical resin and structure. It became a competition and commercial reference, but different seed generations and selected cuts can vary in flowering time, density, citrus, spice, and overall intensity.

cultivation.trainingResponse: Can benefit from topping and spreading

confidence.notes: Core family ancestry is widely published; release and cut differences remain.

name: Neville's Haze
breeder: The Seed Bank / Green House historical context

parentage.notes: Historical naming and parental shorthand vary by source.

overview: Neville's Haze is a long-flowering Haze-dominant landmark prized by experienced growers for incense, spice, electric intensity, and breeding value. It demands space, patience, and careful selection. Different releases and preserved lines can vary substantially, so historical labels alone do not guarantee the same plant population.

confidence.notes: Family direction is clear; detailed parental shorthand and surviving versions require verification.

name: Northern Lights #5 × Haze
overview: NL5/Haze is a landmark pairing that brought the height, incense, and cerebral intensity of Haze into a resinous Northern Lights framework. It became a parent family as much as a single product. Seed source, generation, Haze parent, and selected cut all matter when comparing records.

confidence.notes: Cross label is clear, while specific parent versions and releases vary.

name: Hindu Kush

parentage.notes: The name covers distinct regional and commercial lines.

overview: Hindu Kush is a regional family name associated with compact mountain-adapted plants, broad leaves, resin, and earthy hash-spice profiles. Commercial examples can represent very different preservation and selection histories, so this umbrella record should be replaced by accession-level detail whenever it is available.

confidence.notes: Regional identity is broader than a single modern strain.

name: Bubba Kush

parentage.notes: Common stories involve an Afghan-type plant and a named Bubba selection, but accounts differ.

overview: Bubba Kush is a well-known clone family associated with coffee, cocoa, earth, compact growth, and a dense relaxing character. Several cuts and seed lines use the name. Reliable records should specify Pre-98, another authenticated cut, or the exact seed source instead of treating every Bubba as identical.

confidence.notes: Family traits are familiar, but exact ancestry and cut identity vary.

name: Purple Kush

parentage.display: Commonly reported as Hindu Kush × Purple Afghani
parentage.notes: Multiple versions share the name.

overview: Purple Kush is a name applied to purple, broad-leaf Kush-family plants prized for dense flowers, grape-earth aroma, resin, and a strong evening profile. Since multiple cuts and seed versions circulate, the source and exact parent claims should be documented for any serious breeding record.

confidence.notes: The name covers multiple versions.

name: Granddaddy Purple

parentage.notes: Widely reported shorthand; commercial versions vary.

overview: Granddaddy Purple became a widely recognized purple hybrid name associated with dark berry, grape, compact flowers, and a heavy evening direction. Its commercial success produced many seed lines and look-alikes, making source documentation important for archival and breeding use.

confidence.notes: Core shorthand is common; exact commercial versions differ.

name: Blue Dream

parentage.notes: The name has been applied to more than one cut and seed version.

overview: Blue Dream became a highly visible West Coast hybrid name associated with berry, haze, vigor, yield, and an accessible uplifted profile. Its popularity means provenance is unusually important: a verified cut, a seed recreation, and an unrelated name-use may perform very differently.

confidence.notes: Name reuse and multiple versions complicate exact traits.

name: AK-47

parentage.notes: Published family shorthand does not capture the full selection history.

overview: AK-47 is a long-running Serious Seeds polyhybrid known for pungent sweet spice, resin, broad hybrid effects, and practical indoor performance. Its stable brand identity is stronger than the precision of simplified four-region pedigree summaries, which should be treated as family context rather than a complete breeding ledger.

confidence.notes: Breeder-published family origins are available; exact selections remain proprietary.

name: White Widow

parentage.notes: Breeder attribution and exact parental history are disputed among historical accounts.

overview: White Widow is a foundational Dutch-era resin cultivar name associated with frosted flowers, sharp floral spice, and balanced hybrid effects. Competing historical accounts and many later commercial versions make original attribution and exact ancestry contentious, even though its market and breeding influence are beyond dispute.

confidence.notes: Influence is clear; authorship and detailed origin remain contested.

name: Girl Scout Cookies

parentage.display: Commonly reported as OG Kush × F1 Durban
parentage.notes: Cut history and parent labels vary across accounts.

overview: GSC became a defining modern clone family with sweet dough, mint, earth, fuel, dense resin, and major breeding influence. Several named cuts and many seed descendants exist, so exact cut identity matters. Parent shorthand is widely repeated but not equally documented in every source.

confidence.notes: Cut and parent details vary; record exact source.

name: GG4

parentage.display: Sour Dubb × Chem's Sister × Chocolate Diesel-associated cross
parentage.notes: Published descriptions differ on how the multi-parent shorthand is sequenced.

overview: GG4 is a modern clone landmark known for sticky resin, sour chemical fuel, vigor, and strong effects. It is a specific selection from a complex cross, but the name has been used loosely in commerce. Breeding records should distinguish the authenticated clone, selfed seed, and hybrid descendants.

confidence.notes: Cross family is documented; shorthand sequencing can differ.

name: Wedding Cake

parentage.notes: The Wedding Cake name has also been used for other products in some markets.

overview: Wedding Cake is a modern dessert-and-fuel clone name associated with dense resin, doughy sweetness, pepper, and strong layered effects. Naming overlap and the broad commercial use of 'Cake' make exact source, clone label, and breeder context especially important.

confidence.notes: Names and cut labels overlap across markets.

name: Gelato #33
overview: Gelato #33 is a selected cut from the Gelato family known for creamy fruit, sweet gas, color, resin, and a strong modern hybrid profile. Gelato numbering identifies particular selections, but commercial labeling is inconsistent, so cut provenance should be stored with the record.

confidence.notes: Selection numbering is meaningful but often used inconsistently.

name: Zkittlez

parentage.notes: Some published versions include an additional undisclosed contribution.

overview: Zkittlez became a modern aroma benchmark for layered fruit candy, citrus, grape, and unusually persistent flavor. Its breeding value is often discussed in terms of sensory intensity rather than raw structure or yield. Exact parent descriptions and selected versions should be verified against breeder material.

confidence.notes: Aroma reputation is strong; exact pedigree descriptions vary.

name: Runtz
overview: Runtz is a modern candy-and-cream hybrid name associated with sweet fruit, gas, color, and resin. Rapid commercial adoption produced many unrelated or loosely related versions. An archive-quality entry should name the breeder, seed lot or cut, and whether the record concerns the original selection or a descendant.

confidence.notes: Original identity and commercial name use need separation.

name: Tangie
overview: Tangie helped re-establish intense tangerine peel and sweet citrus as a major modern breeding direction. It is often vigorous, aromatic, and energetic, with meaningful phenotype variation in structure, flower density, and the balance of orange versus skunk character.

confidence.notes: Breeder and release histories should be checked for the specific version.

name: Trainwreck

parentage.display: Northern California clone of disputed ancestry
parentage.notes: Mexican, Thai, and Afghani-associated ancestry is often repeated but not conclusively documented.

overview: Trainwreck is a classic Northern California clone name associated with lemon, pine, spice, lanky growth, resin, and a forceful fast cerebral onset. Origin stories and seed recreations vary. The authenticated clone should be distinguished from Trainwreck-named commercial lines.

confidence.notes: Clone reputation is established; ancestry is not verified.

name: Maui Wowie

parentage.notes: Many unrelated or hybridized commercial versions share the name.

overview: Maui Wowie is a historic Hawaiian cultivar name associated with tropical fruit, floral sweetness, tall outdoor growth, and an upbeat character. Because the name became a global cultural shorthand, a modern record needs collection, breeder, and generation details before making specific genetic claims.

confidence.notes: Historical name is clear; modern genetic identity is often uncertain.

name: Acapulco Gold
overview: Acapulco Gold is a historic Mexican regional cultivar name famed in cannabis culture for golden flowers, warm spice, sweetness, and lively effects. Modern products using the name may be recreations or unrelated hybrids. Serious preservation records require origin and accession documentation.

confidence.notes: Historical identity is broad; modern provenance needs verification.

name: Colombian Gold
overview: Colombian Gold is a broad historic name for Colombian cannabis associated with long flowering, incense, sweet wood, citrus, and major contribution to early hybrid breeding. Regional differences and later preservation lines matter; the name alone is not an accession.

confidence.notes: Umbrella record; replace with regional accession detail.

name: Panama Red
overview: Panama Red is a historic Central American cultivar name associated with long flowering, red pistils, spice, earth, and energetic to dreamy effects. Commercial reproductions and preserved lines may differ substantially, so origin and maintenance history are essential data fields.

confidence.notes: Historical name is broad and modern provenance varies.

name: Headband

parentage.notes: Several cuts and seed lines share the name.

overview: Headband is a fuel-family hybrid name generally linked to OG Kush and Sour Diesel. It is associated with lemon-diesel aroma, stretch, resin, and a strong immersive profile. Naming overlap means the exact cut or breeder version should always accompany the record.

confidence.notes: Multiple versions share the name.

name: NYC Diesel

parentage.notes: Published descriptions vary and should be checked against breeder archives.

overview: NYC Diesel is a Soma-associated cultivar known for grapefruit, lime, fuel, and an outgoing hybrid profile. It should not automatically be conflated with East Coast Sour Diesel. Breeder-era seed descriptions and current reproductions may differ.

confidence.notes: Distinct from ECSD; exact breeder shorthand needs archive verification.

name: GMO
overview: GMO is a selected modern clone noted for savory garlic-onion funk, fuel, resin, stretch, and a long, heavy profile. The specific cut is more meaningful than generic Garlic Cookies labeling, and later hybrids or seed reproductions should be tracked separately.

confidence.notes: Clone selection history is broadly documented; commercial naming remains inconsistent.

name: Chem's Sister

parentage.notes: Exact parents are unknown.

overview: Chem's Sister is a historic Chem-family clone with chemical fuel, earth, and breeding significance, including its presence in the ancestry of GG4. Like other Chem selections, the authenticated cut history is clearer than a conventional genetic pedigree.

confidence.notes: Named cut history is recognized; genetic parentage is unknown.

name: Strawberry Cough

parentage.display: Commonly reported as Strawberry Fields × Haze-associated
parentage.notes: Origin stories and commercial versions differ.

overview: Strawberry Cough is associated with fresh strawberry, floral haze, open growth, and a social daytime profile. The name covers a famous clone history as well as later seed lines, so exact source and version should be recorded before assigning detailed breeding conclusions.

confidence.notes: Multiple versions and origin accounts exist.

name: Romulan
overview: Romulan is an old-school clone family associated with pine, earth, spice, resin, and a deep inward profile. Several origin accounts and later seed lines exist. Its importance as a Space Queen parent makes cut identity and source especially relevant in breeding archives.

confidence.notes: Clone family is recognized; exact ancestry is contested.

name: Lamb's Bread

parentage.notes: The name is used broadly and may describe multiple Jamaican selections.

overview: Lamb's Bread is a historic Jamaican cultivar name associated with herbal spice, uplifting character, and cultural importance. It should be treated as a regional or heirloom label unless the accession, community, and preservation history are documented.

confidence.notes: Broad historical name; accession-level verification required.

name: Space Bomb

parentage.notes: Confirm exact release notes before publication.

overview: Space Bomb is a TGA-era Space Queen family hybrid remembered for fruit, spice, resin, and compact-to-branchy hybrid expressions. This starter record is included primarily to support the Glass Grenade project lineage and needs breeder-archive review.
